name: Sensitive Data & Secret Detection
description: Tier A (fileEdited): Checks for hardcoded secrets and PII in code. Fires immediately — a secret in code is dangerous from the moment it's written.

patterns: {DERIVE: application code patterns from tech-stack.md + module-structure.md — e.g., src/**/*.cs, src/**/*.ts, src/**/*.json — PLUS root config files: *.env, appsettings*.json, *.yaml. NEVER bare **/*.ext. See common/hook-preamble.md for Layer 2 runtime filter.}

then.prompt:
## Package Territory Check (MANDATORY — evaluate FIRST)
Before performing any checks below, verify the triggering file path.

EXCLUDED ZONES (package infrastructure — NOT compliance subjects):
• .kiro/hooks/ — AI-GCE hook infrastructure
• .kiro/steering/ — AI-DWG/AI-GCE steering infrastructure
• .kiro/agents/ — AI-GCE agent infrastructure
• .governance/ — AI-GCE governance rules/agents/logs
• compliance-log/ — AI-GCE audit trail
• project-initiation/ — AI-PILC output
• architecture/ — AI-ADLC output
• management_framework/ — Shared governance spine
• docs/compliance-* — AI-GCE generated dashboards
• templates/ — AI-DWG planning templates

If the triggering file's path starts with ANY of the above prefixes:
→ EXIT SILENTLY. Do NOT check, do NOT log, do NOT warn.

If the file is NOT in an excluded zone → proceed with checks below.

---

SECURITY CHECK (Tier A — immediate enforcement):

1. HARDCODED SECRETS: Scan this file for potential secrets:
   - Connection strings with passwords
   - API keys or tokens (patterns: sk-, xoxb-, ghp_, glpat-, key-, token-)
   - Hardcoded credentials (username/password pairs)
   - Private keys or certificates
   - JWT secrets

2. PII IN LOGS/STRINGS: If this file contains logging statements or string outputs, check against .kiro/steering/observability-sensitive.md for data that MUST NOT appear (passwords, SSN, credit cards, etc.).

If secrets found: flag as 🔴 CRITICAL — suggest using environment variables or secret manager.
If PII exposure found: flag as 🟠 HIGH — suggest masking.
If all rules pass, confirm compliance silently.

## Compliance Logging
After completing all checks, append to `compliance-log/events/{today-date}.jsonl`:
{"timestamp": "{ISO-8601-UTC}", "type": "check", "id": "chk-{date}-{time}-{seq}", "hook": "sensitive-data-check", "trigger": "fileEdited", "ruleId": "SEC-BASELINE-01", "ruleSeverity": "critical", "result": "{pass|fail|warn}", "message": "{finding}", "sessionDedup": true}

If a previous log entry exists for the same hook + file path within this session, OVERWRITE it.
